content: The linguistic double meaning of "reflexion" (both the physical optical reflection of light and the cognitive act of deliberate reflection) can serve as a structural metaphor to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms. Just as physical reflexion bounces light off a surface to generate a visible image of an object from a new angle, multi-perspective LLM self-reflection can bounce the model’s own output off an external frame of reference (a human-defined evaluation metric, another model’s reasoning, or established domain facts) to generate a clearer, more accurate critique of its initial work.
